title: Pixagraph [Retired]
description: Semi-Realistic Light Fantasy | 1.13 - 1.19 | 16x16 | Optifine Connected Texture Support!
body:
**Hi everywhere! It's Amzahr again, and I am here with a new resource pack! This pack is a 16x16 RPG Pixel Graphic Gaming inspired to take to the vanilla Minecraft resources. This resource pack includes alternate textures (1.13+ only) for decorative blocks and has support for optifine connected textures.**
 
 ---
 
**PROJECT STATUS: 1.19+ and 22w42a Snapshot RELEASED!!**

<details> 
<summary>IMPORTANT UPDATE: AN HONEST DISCUSSION ABOUT THE FUTURE OF PIXAGRAPH
</summary>

An Honest Discussion about the future of Pixagraph


As many who follow me have probably noticed the updates on Pixagraph have nearly slowed to a halt, when they used to be shortly after snapshots were released. Many factors play into this. I want to share the reasons of why.

Originally I started working on Pixagraph the summer between high school and college. Which was in 2017. It was a hobby I never expected to blow up nearly as much as it did. It's 2023 now, I have graduated from collage, have a full time job now, I am engaged, have legally changed my name and gender markers, I've moved to a completely different state etc, etc etc.

However, the biggest set back on updating the pack has been artistic vision. As previously mentioned I started this when I was basically fresh out of high school. I studied in art and have a BFA now. My artistic style and knowledge has changed so much. When I open Pixagraph now I run into the issue of having to us a much more amateur art style that is frankly, kind of difficult for me to force nowadays. I've thought about retexturing stuff but then that requires cleaning up the pack all the way back to 1.13. Which is simply something I do not have the time or energy for.

What does this mean? I am going to be officially retiring Pixagraph, it will still be up for download and everything but will not be updated passed it's current version. That does not mean I am retiring myself. In fact far from it!! I am actually currently working on a spiritual successor to Pixagraph that fits my current style. This is a sneak peak at it. Thank you everyone for this wonderful trip and I hope you enjoy what is something!

 </details>


**Note: This is the only official download of Pixagraph: if you are download this resource pack through different means than Modrinth, it is not an official download. Please be smart and safe with your downloads!**
  
---

**Terms of Use (Please Read Before Using):**

**Do:**

-If used for a video, please link back for this Thread. 

-Edit Textures for your Private Usage. 

-Use this as an official Resource Pack for your server. Again, Link back to this Thread. 

-Share with your friends 

**Do Not:**

-Reupload any of the textures found in this pack. 

-Modified any of the textures found in this pack. 

-Use it in your Pack without Permission. 


Last, but not least. I'd like to thank all my friends who have supported and encouraged me while making this Resource Pack. It was a lot of fun and I couldn't have done it without you guys.
And to the fans to have supported and helped me find issues and gave feedback on the pack, without you guys I wouldn't be able to get any of this done.

Credit to Sildur for the shader pack used in the images
[Shader Pack Used can be found here](https://sildurs-shaders.github.io/)
